message.content:
<|channel>thought<channel|>### C# API Design: Resumable Benchmark Runner

To satisfy the requirement of a **resumable** runner operating within a **single-server Docker harness**, the design must prioritize a sequential execution queue with a persistent state (to track completed vs. pending tasks).

#### 1. Core API Implementation

```csharp
using System.Collections.Concurrent;

public record BenchmarkResult(double Score, DateTime Timestamp, string Status);

public interface IBenchmarkRunner
{
    Task RunAllAsync(CancellationToken ct);
    Task RunSingleAsync(BenchmarkContext context, CancellationToken ct);
}

public class LocalModelBenchmarkRunner : IBenchmarkRunner
{
    private readonly IBenchmarkRepository _repository; // Persistence layer (SQL/JSON)
    private readonly IDockerHarness _harness;           // Docker orchestration
    private readonly SemaphoreSlim _executionLock = new(1, 1);

    public LocalModelBenchmarkRunner(IBenchmarkRepository repository, IDockerHarness harness)
    {
        _repository = repository;
        _harness = harness;
    }

    public async Task RunAllAsync(CancellationToken ct)
    {
        // Resumability: Fetch only tasks not marked 'Completed'
        var pendingTasks = await _repository.GetPendingContextsAsync();
        
        foreach (var context in pendingTasks)
        {
            if (ct.IsCancellationRequested) break;
            await RunSingleAsync(context, ct);
        }
    }

    public async Task RunSingleAsync(BenchmarkContext context, CancellationToken ct)
    {
        // Ensure only one heavy model server runs at a time
        await _executionLock.WaitAsync(ct);
        try
        {
            // Watchdog/Timeout implementation
